docs.title: Eqvaldi Linux
docs.text:
• Download

    Get the latest stable, edge, or rolling release for your platform.

    Downloads

  • Build it yourself

    Full source available. Build your own image with the provided scripts and build server.

    Building guide

  • Changelog

    Track every release across all LTS lines from V1 through V4.

    Changelog

  • News

    Latest announcements and updates from the project.

    News

docs.title: What is Eqvaldi Linux?
docs.text: Eqvaldi is a minimal, customized Linux distribution based on Debian and Armbian, targeting both x86 and ARM single-board computers. It ships a carefully selected package set to stay lean while still being usable out of the box.

docs.title: Key characteristics
docs.text: Feature Details Base Debian (Bookworm / Bullseye) + Armbian Desktops Cinnamon, MATE Architectures x86_64, ARM (various SBCs) Release lines V2-LTS · V3-LTS · V4-dev Kernel LTS + Edge options

docs.title: Release lines
docs.text:
V4-LTSV3-LTSV2-LTS (EOL)V1-LTS (EOL)

Latest development builds targeting Debian 13 (Trixie) and Armbian 24.8. Rolling updates, not production-ready.

Stable long-term support on Debian 12 (Bookworm). Recommended for most users.

Previous long-term support on Debian 11 (Bullseye). Maintenance-only.

End-of-life. No longer receives updates.

Download V3-LTS View Source

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: Full release history across all release lines.

docs.title: V4-LTS
docs.text:
Release4.0 (V4-LTS)

  • Replace spek with sox.
  • Add new image variant: minimal (no Desktop Env.)
  • Add new image variant: KDE
  • Bump Armbian to 26.02.0
  • Bump Debian to 13.3
  • Bunp linux kernels to 2026.2.4.
  • Replace neofetch with hyfech.
  • Add pakages :libsdl2-net-dev spek orchis-gtk-theme libsndfile1-dev.
  • Remove Jetson Nano and Debian 12 builds.
  • Remove pakages :qtbase5-dev qtbase5-private-dev qtbase5-dev-tools qttools5-dev
  • LXQT replaces LXDE.

docs.title: V3-LTS
docs.text:
Release3.6 (V3-LTS)

  • Bump Armbian to 25.11
  • Bump Debian to 12.12
  • Bunp linux kernels to 2025.11.23.
  • Add pakages :eject libzip-dev zipcmp zipmerge ziptool libsdl2-mixer-dev
  • Add numix icon theme
  • Remove Jetson Nano builds
  • Remove pakages :qtbase5-dev qtbase5-private-dev qtbase5-dev-tools qttools5-dev

docs.title: Release 3.5.063024-dev
docs.text:

  • Bump Linux kernels to 2024.6.30
  • Add packages: eject, libzip-dev, zipcmp, zipmerge, ziptool, libsdl2-mixer-dev
  • Add Numix icon theme

docs.title: Release 3.3.100623
docs.text:
• Bump Armbian to 23.11
  • Bump Debian to 12.2

docs.title: Release 3.2.080623
docs.text:

docs.text:

  • Move to Bookworm (Debian 12)
  • Bump Linux kernel versions: 6.1.xx, 5.4.242, EDGE 6.3.x (Current)

docs.title: Release 2.8.063024-dev
docs.text:
• Bump Armbian to 24.8

docs.title: Release 2.7.100623
docs.text:

docs.title: Release 2.6.080623
docs.text: • Bump Armbian to 23.08

docs.title: Release 2.5.053023
docs.text:
• Bump Armbian to 23.05
  • Bump Debian to 11.7
  • Add packages: bash-completion, alsa-utils, apt-utils, sudo, alsa-firmware-loaders, cifs-utils

• Move to minimal from standard Debian

docs.title: V1-LTS (EOL)
docs.text:
End of Life

V1-LTS no longer receives updates or security fixes.

docs.title: Release 1.8
docs.text:
• Add packages: git, nasm, libgl1-mesa-dev, libsdl2-dev, libflac-dev, libvpx-dev, libgtk2.0-dev, freepats, ninja-build, qtbase5-dev, qtbase5-private-dev, qtbase5-dev-tools, qttools5-dev
  • Bump Armbian to 22.11.0
  • Bump Debian to 11.5
  • Bump Linux kernel versions: 5.15.74, 5.19.16, 5.10.147, 5.4.218 (Current)
  • Remove legacy kernel builds
  • Add more SBCs to Cinnamon
  • Revert to Chromium as default web browser

docs.title: Release 1.1
docs.text:
• Remove XFCE4 power-manager and power-manager-plugins (MATE Desktop)
  • Add Cinnamon DE
  • Add MATE power manager (MATE Desktop)
  • Bump Linux kernel versions: 5.15.48, 5.17.15, 5.4.199, 5.10.123 (Current)
  • Bump Debian to 11.4

docs.title: Release 1.0
docs.text: • Initial release

docs.title: Downloads
docs.text: Three release channels are available. Choose the one that fits your needs.

docs.title: Release channels
docs.text:
StableEdgeRolling

Production-ready builds. Recommended for daily use.

Download Stable

Builds with newer kernels, but less tested. Good for hardware that requires recent drivers.

Download Edge

Latest development snapshots built from the dev branch. May be unstable.

Download Rolling

docs.title: Supported platforms
docs.text: Platform V2-LTS V3-LTS V4-dev x86_64 ARM64 ARMHF Jetson Nano EOL

docs.title: Verifying downloads
docs.text:
After downloading, verify your image before flashing.

sha256sum Eqvaldi-*.img.xz

Compare the output against the checksum file published alongside the release on GitHub.

news stuff

docs.title: Building Overview
docs.text: Eqvaldi provides everything you need to build your own images from source. There are two main paths: the build server (automated, server-side) and install scripts (post-install configuration on a running system).

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/Eqvaldi/build.git
cd build
./compile.sh

See the build server repository for full documentation on compile flags, supported boards, and kernel selection.

docs.title: Build paths
docs.text:
• Build Server

    The primary build system. Produces .img.xz images for all supported platforms.

    Build Server guide

  • Install Scripts

    Post-install scripts for configuring a fresh Debian system with the Eqvaldi package set.

    Install Scripts guide

docs.title: Source repositories
docs.text: Repository Description Eqvaldi/build Main build environment (images) Eqvaldi/Debian-build-server-V2 Automated build server Eqvaldi/install-scripts Debian post-install scripts Eqvaldi/releases Release artifacts

docs.title: Build Server
docs.text: The Eqvaldi Debian build server (V2) is the automated pipeline used to produce all official image releases.

docs.title: Repository
docs.text: Eqvaldi/Debian-build-server-V2

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Debian or Ubuntu host machine (x86_64 recommended)
  • Docker or a compatible container runtime
  • At least 20 GB of free disk space
  • Git

docs.title: Building an image
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/Eqvaldi/Debian-build-server-V2.git
cd Debian-build-server-V2
# Edit config.sh to select board, release line, and kernel flavour
./build.sh

The output image will be placed in the output/ directory as a compressed .img.xz file.

docs.title: Selecting a kernel flavour
docs.text:
Flavour Description current Recommended LTS kernel edge Latest mainline kernel — more hardware support, less tested legacy Older LTS kernel — hardware-specific, use only if required

Warning

Legacy kernel builds were removed in V1.8. Do not use them on new installations.

docs.title: Install Scripts
docs.text: Post-install scripts for configuring a fresh Debian system with the Eqvaldi package set and customizations.

docs.title: Repository
docs.text: Eqvaldi/install-scripts

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Clone the repository onto a freshly installed Debian system and run the appropriate script for your target:

git clone https://github.com/Eqvaldi/install-scripts.git
cd install-scripts
bash install.sh

Tip

Run the install scripts as root or with sudo. Review each script before executing to understand what packages and configurations will be applied.

docs.title: What the scripts do
docs.text:
• Install the Eqvaldi package set (desktop, multimedia, utilities)
  • Apply system configuration defaults
  • Set up the Cinnamon or MATE desktop environment
  • Configure kernel and firmware packages appropriate for your hardware
